questions.q: A set containing 3 elements is said to be a ______ set?
questions.explanation: A finite set is defined as a set that has a finite number of elements, i.e., the number of elements in the set is countable. In this case, a set containing 3 elements is a finite set.

questions.q: The set of integers less than -5 is denoted by?
questions.explanation: The set of integers less than -5 is denoted by {x: x ∈ Z, x < -5} because it includes all integers x that are less than -5.

questions.q: The number of elements in the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} is?
questions.explanation: The number of elements in a set is determined by counting the individual elements. In this case, the set {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6} has 6 elements.

questions.q: The union of two sets A and B is denoted by?
questions.explanation: The union of two sets A and B is denoted by A ∪ B. It includes all elements that are in A, in B, or in both A and B.

questions.q: The intersection of two sets A and B is denoted by?
questions.explanation: The intersection of two sets A and B is denoted by A ∩ B. It includes all elements that are common to both A and B.
The intersection of A and B is the set of elements common to both. In this case, the only element common to both sets is 3.
The difference of A and B is the set of elements that are in A but not in B. In this case, the elements 1 and 2 are in A but not in B.

questions.q: A relation R from set A to set B is a subset of A × B. (True/False)
questions.explanation: A relation R from set A to set B is indeed a subset of A × B, as it is a collection of ordered pairs (a, b) where a is in A and b is in B.

questions.q: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {a, b, c}, then A × B =?
questions.explanation: The Cartesian product of A and B is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a is in A and b is in B.

questions.q: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {a, b, c}, then A ∪ B =?
questions.explanation: The union of A and B is the set of all elements that are in A, in B, or in both A and B.

questions.q: Show that (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∩ B) = A ∩ B.
questions.explanation: To show that (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∩ B) = A ∩ B, we need to prove that (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∩ B) ⊆ A ∩ B and A ∩ B ⊆ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∩ B).
To find (A - B) ∪ (B - A), we first need to find A - B and B - A, and then find their union.
To find (A ∩ B) - B, we first need to find A ∩ B, and then find the difference of A ∩ B and B.
To find (A - B) ∩ B, we first need to find A - B, and then find the intersection of A - B and B.

questions.q: A relation R from set A to set B is said to be _______ if it is reflexive and symmetric.
questions.explanation: A relation R from set A to set B is an equivalence relation if it is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
To find A × (B - A), we first need to find B - A, and then find the Cartesian product of A and B - A.
To find A × (B ∪ A), we first need to find B ∪ A, and then find the Cartesian product of A and B ∪ A.
